description: Non-contiguous shared group: master B1 (=A1*2), clones B2 and D5. ts-xlsx plans the ref as master->furthest-clone (B1:D5), leaving 11 empty interior cells inside the bounding box. Probes whether Excel materializes them, ignores them, or repairs the package.
verdict: BENIGN. Excel treats a shared-formula `ref` as a bounding-box hint, not an assertion that every enclosed cell is a clone. It opened the sparse-ref package without repair, did not materialize the 11 empty interior cells, and re-saved a byte-structurally identical group (same ref=B1:D5, same si=0, the same two clones). ts-xlsx's planSharedFormulas output for a non-contiguous group is already Excel's own canonical form, so splitting into contiguous runs or degrading clones to standalone <f> would make ts-xlsx DIVERGE from Excel. Lock with a Tier-2 seam fact asserting our emitted ref matches this canonical ref.
